title: Calo Nord
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}} {{Update|[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]}} {{Character |type=Bounty hunter |image=[[File:Calo_Nord_profile.png]] |name=Calo Nord |homeworld= |birth= |death=[[3956 BBY]],''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]'' [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]]''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]''{{SW|url=news/so-uncivilized-great-gunslingers-in-star-wars-part-1|series=So Uncivilized: Great Gunslingers in Star Wars|text=Part 1|nolive=1}} |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]]{{DB|character|calonord|Nord, Calo}} |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=new |height=1.5 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes= |skin=Light |cyber= |families= |parents=*[[Calo Nord's father|Father]] *[[Calo Nord's mother|Mother]] |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation=*[[Exchange]] *[[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] *[[Darth Revan's Sith Empire|Sith Empire]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|I have to give you credit. You've led me on quite a chase. But nobody gets away from Calo Nord in the end.|Calo Nord, to Revan|audio=NGLOBECALO02000.flac|}} '''Calo Nord''' was an infamous [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] who lived during the [[Jedi Civil War]], a [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]]-spanning conflict initiated by the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lords]] [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] [[Revan/Legends|Revan]] and [[Darth Malak]]. Nord was sold into [[Slavery/Legends|slavery]] by his [[Calo Nord's mother|mother]] and [[Calo Nord's father|father]] at a young age. On his sixteenth [[Birthday/Legends|birthday]], he [[Death/Legends|killed]] his slave masters and tracked down his parents, murdering them as well. A [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] was placed on Nord, but he eliminated every [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] that pursued him. Once the attempts on his life ended, Nord murdered those who had placed the bounty and became a bounty hunter by trade, taking assignments from any party that paid. Nord occasionally worked for the [[Exchange]] [[Crime lord/Legends|criminal syndicate boss]] [[Davik Kang]] but had been also contracted by the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] for operations in the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core]]. During the [[3956 BBY|final year]] of the Jedi Civil War, Nord was on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Taris/Legends|Taris]] for business with Kang. Following the [[Attack on the Endar Spire|attack]] on the Republic vessel ''[[Endar Spire]], ''Malak, who had [[Capture of Darth Revan|betrayed]] Revan and usurped control of the [[Darth Revan's Sith Empire|Sith Empire]], quarantined the planet in order to search for the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Bastila Shan]], who had become marooned on the Tarisian surface. Malak eventually ordered the planet's [[Destruction of Taris|destruction]] in an attempt to kill Shan. When the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] began to [[Orbital bombardment/Legends|bombard]] Taris, Nord and Kang attempted to flee aboard Kang's [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] ''[[Ebon Hawk]]'' and caught Shan and her companions during their [[Rescue of Bastila Shan|attempt]] to steal Kang's flagship. A firefight ensued, which ended when Nord was caught under falling debris and trapped. The bounty hunter was able to flee Taris and evade the devastation wrought by the Sith. He was subsequently hired by Darth Malak to track down Shan and capture her alive. Nord located Shan's companion, Revan—now a [[Jedi training/Legends|partially retrained]] [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]]—in the [[Eastern Dune Sea]] on the desert planet of [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] as he was searching for the ancient [[Rakata/Legends|Rakatan]] [[Space station/Legends|space station]] known as the [[Star Forge]]. As Revan and his team exited some [[Rakatan Ruins (Tatooine)|ancient ruins]], the bounty hunter [[Ambush on Tatooine (Jedi Civil War)|confronted]] Revan. Nord was overmatched by Revan and his companions, resulting in his death. ==Biography== ===Early life and bounty hunter=== {{Quote|Calo Nord's one of the most famous bounty hunters in the galaxy. He's killed more people than the Iridian Plague!|Mission Vao|}} [[File:Calo_Nord.png|thumb|left|250px|Calo Nord on Taris]] When the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] known as Calo Nord was a young boy, he was sold into [[Slavery/Legends|slavery]] by his [[Calo Nord's mother|mother]] and [[Calo Nord's father|father]]. During his time as a slave, Nord learned to be ruthless and cold-hearted. After [[Birthday/Legends|turning]] sixteen [[Time/Legends|years]] of age, Nord [[Death/Legends|butchered]] his slave masters. Free, he located his parents and murdered them. A [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] was placed on Nord's head, and the young man was pursued by many [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]]. Nord observed the means and methodologies of the bounty hunters who tracked him, learning and acquiring trade skills. He used his new-found skills to eliminate each one that was in pursuit. Eventually, the attempts on his life ceased.[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic'': Prima's Official Strategy Guide]] {{C|Under ''Calo Nord''}} The [[Bounty Hunters' Guild/Legends|Bounty Hunters' Guild]] had deemed Nord too dangerous and cancelled the contract. Nord, however, found and killed the people that had placed the bounty. Nord became a bounty hunter himself, taking contracts from whoever paid him. Among these contracts were operations on behalf of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] in the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core]]. As a bounty hunter, Nord commissioned the building of [[Calo Nord's battle armor|personal armor]], based on the [[Mandalorian armor/Legends|design]] worn by the [[Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders/Legends|Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders]]. Following its completion, Nord killed [[Unidentified armorer (Calo Nord)|the maker]] of the armor to make sure that it would be unique to him, and also to keep from paying the designer. He occasionally worked for [[Davik Kang]], a [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] of the [[Exchange]] crime syndicate whose operations were based on the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Ecumenopolis/Legends|ecumenopolis]] of [[Taris/Legends|Taris]]. When contracted, Kang hired Nord to kill and bring back the heads of [[Rancor/Legends|rancors]] so that the crime lord could mount the heads in his trophy room.''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'', [[Calo Nord's datapad|Datapad]] containing records of Nord's trophy kills Among the locations Nord visited while on Taris was the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] [[Zax]]'s [[Zax's bounty office|bounty office]], an establishment contained within the [[Lower City]] locale of [[Javyar's Cantina]]. By the time of the [[Jedi Civil War]], a [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]]-spanning conflict initiated by [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] [[Revan/Legends|Revan]], [[Darth Malak]], and their [[Darth Revan's Sith Empire|Sith Empire]], it had even been said that Nord had killed more people than the [[Iridian Plague/Legends|Iridian Plague]].''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'', [[Revan/Legends|Revan's]] conversation with [[Mission Vao]] in [[Javyar's Cantina]] ===Jedi Civil War=== ====Destruction of Taris==== {{Quote|A Jedi and a war hero—it's a wonder you survived the encounter.''"
"''I am hard to kill, Lord Malak.|Darth Malak and Calo Nord|}} In [[3957 BBY]], Darth Malak usurped the mantle of [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] when he betrayed his [[Sith Master/Legends|Sith Master]] Revan as the Jedi and Republic forces tried to [[Capture of Darth Revan|capture]] them both.{{DB|character|darthmalak|Malak, Darth}} [[3956 BBY|One year later]], Malak [[Capture of Taris|conquered]] Taris; and, after the Jedi Knight [[Bastila Shan]] crash-landed on Taris, the Dark Lord quarantined the planet and ordered his [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] to [[Blockade/Legends|blockade]] the planet so that the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] forces could search for Shan. [[File:Malak_hires_Nord.png|thumb|right|250px|Darth Malak hires Calo Nord to capture Bastila Shan.]] During the Sith's quarantine of Taris, Nord, who was on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] for business with Davik Kang, encountered a Republic [[Soldier/Legends|soldier]] named Revan on two occasions. Revan, who was unknowingly the former Dark Lord of the Sith, had been [[Attack on the Endar Spire|marooned]] on Taris with Republic [[Commander/Legends|commander]] [[Carth Onasi]], and they were [[Rescue of Bastila Shan|searching]] for Shan. Nord first met Revan in Javyar's Cantina, where the bounty hunter killed three [[Black Vulkars/Legends|Black Vulkars]] for having disturbed him. Revan tried to engage Nord in conversation, but the departing Nord desired not to be bothered. Revan complied, not wanting to provoke the bounty hunter into attacking him. Nord later encountered Revan when the soldier was exploring the Lower City's apartments. There, Nord killed two [[Rodian/Legends|Rodians]]—the assassin [[Luugro]] and [[Luugro's partner|his companion]]—so that he could collect the corresponding bounties. Nord was present at [[Davik Kang's estate|Kang's estate]] when the crime lord's enforcer, the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Canderous Ordo]], brought Revan, Onasi, and Shan—who had been successfully rescued by the two Republic soldiers—to the estate. Ordo introduced Revan to the crime lord as an individual interested in joining the Exchange. Nord told the Mandalorian that it was not like him to take on partners and taunted Ordo. He then accompanied Kang while the crime lord treated Revan and his companions to a tour of his estate, showing them his [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]], the ''[[Ebon Hawk]]''. After the tour, Nord and Kang took Ordo, Revan, Shan, and Onasi to a guest room and warned Revan to stay in the guest wing of the estate. Kang left to look into Revan's background, as a standard precaution, and told Nord to come with him. Aboard his [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]] [[Leviathan (Interdictor-class cruiser)|''Leviathan'']], Darth Malak concluded that his troops' search for Shan was taking too long and ordered the Sith [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Saul Karath]] to have the Sith fleet [[Destruction of Taris|destroy]] Taris in order to kill the Jedi. Nord and Kang, hoping to flee the [[Orbital bombardment/Legends|bombardment]], entered the hangar where the ''Ebon Hawk'' was housed, but they discovered that Revan and his companions were attempting to steal the ship. After Kang spoke to the group, Nord told the crime lord he would kill them, stating that he had looked forward to doing so for a long time. Kang implored the bounty hunter to hurry, saying that if they did not escape, the Sith's [[Laser/Legends|lasers]] would be their demise. In the ensuing firefight, Nord realized that he and Kang were outmatched. Kang perished, and Nord—who was not willing to let Ordo, the Republic soldiers and the Jedi escape—tried to use a [[Thermal detonator/Legends|thermal detonator]] to kill everyone, including himself. Instead, the bombardment partially destroyed the hangar, causing Nord to be buried underneath a heap of rubble. Leaving Nord and the deceased Kang, Revan's group boarded the ''Ebon Hawk'' and gathered the rest of their companions before fleeing Taris and the Sith bombardment. ====Death==== {{Quote|You got lucky on Taris; the Sith attack saved you from a quick and gruesome death. But I promise you, the Sith won't be getting in my way this time!"''
"''Hold on, Calo… maybe we can work out a deal here."''
"''Sorry, I'm not in this for the credits. You're the only ones who've ever gotten away from me—I've got a rep to protect!|Calo Nord and Revan|}} [[File:Calonordtat.png|thumb|left|250px|Calo Nord confronts Revan in the Eastern Dune Sea on Tatooine.]] Even though he had been buried in rubble on Taris, Nord survived the attack and was able to escape the planet. He was later granted an audience with Darth Malak aboard the ''Leviathan''. Admiral Karath brought Nord in as an eyewitness of Bastila Shan's survival. When the bounty hunter entered the bridge, Malak expressed his surprise that Nord had managed to survive his encounter with Shan and Onasi, to which the bounty hunter replied that he was difficult to kill. Nord agreed to capture Shan, and Karath assured Malak that the bounty hunter's services were worth the demanded price. Malak told Nord that he desired Shan taken alive, if possible, but that the Jedi's other companions were of no value. Nord and Karath then privately met with Malak away from the crew on the ''Leviathan''{{'s}} bridge, during which Nord revealed to Malak that his former [[Sith Master/Legends|Sith Master]], Revan, had survived and was one of the members responsible for Shan's escape. Nord set out to find both Shan and Revan, who was now a [[Jedi training/Legends|retrained]] Jedi [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] tasked with searching for the mysterious [[Rakata/Legends|Rakatan]] [[Star Forge]] [[Space station/Legends|space station]] under Malak's control. The bounty hunter located Revan in the [[Eastern Dune Sea]] on the desert world [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] and hired two [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] and two [[Aqualish/Legends|Aqualish]] henchmen to confront the elusive Jedi. Nord set an [[Ambush on Tatooine (Jedi Civil War)|ambush]] while Revan was inside a [[Rakatan Ruins (Tatooine)|cave]] where a [[Canyon krayt dragon/Legends|krayt dragon]] had taken nest. Nord confronted Revan after the Jedi found a second Rakatan [[Star Map]], which was needed to locate the Star Forge, inside the cave. Nord gave Revan credit for having evaded him thus far but stated that no one could ultimately escape him. The bounty hunter and his accomplices were overmatched and killed by the former Sith Lord and his companions. ===Legacy=== {{Quote|Calo Nord is dead, Lord Malak. He has failed in his mission. Forgive me."''
"''The penalty for failure is death, Admiral Karath. But the failure was Calo's, not yours. You may rise.|Saul Karath and Darth Malak|audio=SaulMalakCaloDead.flac|}} Shortly after Nord's death, Admiral Karath informed Darth Malak of Nord's failure. Malak told Karath that the penalty for failure was death, but that the failure belonged to Nord, not Karath himself. Malak dispatched his [[Shadow Hand]], [[Darth Bandon]], to kill Revan and capture Shan. Prior to his death, the leaders of the secret bounty hunter guild known as the [[GenoHaradan]] had considered admitting Nord into their organization, although he had not yet proven himself worthy of joining. Due to Nord's death at Revan's hands, however, the GenoHaradan's overseers opted to approach Revan with an invitation instead. Four thousand years after Nord's death, he was primarily remembered as a footnote in Revan's career with his exploits forgotten. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|Go away."''
"''I saw how you mopped up those Black Vulkars. Nice work."''
"''One."''
"''I'm not looking for a fight. I just want to talk."''
"''Two."''
"''Okay, I see your point. I'll be off, then."''
"''Smart.|Calo Nord and Revan|}} [[File:Nord_kills_Sith_trooper.jpg|thumb|right|252x252px|Nord killing a Sith trooper]] Calo Nord was a diminutive Human male who seemed harmless upon a first glance. Nord spoke [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Basic]], the Republic's trade language. He moved with careful, deliberate precision and possessed an expressionless face. He was willing to take contracts from anyone who paid him well. Even though his ultimate loyalty was to whomever offered him the most [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]], Nord also worked for the thrill of the hunt. He enjoyed toying with other mercenaries. Nord had a reputation of being unafraid of potential adversaries, in addition to his regular perception of being cold, menacing, and unapproachable. He also possessed innate instincts for both survival and killing. Nord's story was dismissed by some as a myth that was created to scare aspiring criminals. No name was more feared among the criminals in the Outer Rim than Calo Nord. In a couple cases, when someone disturbed Nord, he counted to three, with a number after each interjection. Having counted to three in order for the being to escape his anger, he opened fire on the nuisance for bothering him. Nord was not willing to let anyone defeat him and would go to great lengths to ensure that an individual never did. When Shan, Ordo, and Revan overpowered him on Taris, Nord threatened to kill himself with a thermal detonator in an attempt to ensure their deaths. The bounty hunter also aspired to claim a [[Krayt dragon pearl/Legends|pearl]] from a krayt dragon on Tatooine. He considered one of these pearls to be the greatest of hunting trophies. When Calo Nord was a boy, he was sold into slavery by his parents. He murdered his slave masters when he turned sixteen and tracked down his parents, murdering them. After establishing himself as a bounty hunter, Nord worked for any individual who offered him the most credits. He had working business relationships with Exchange crime lord Davik Kang, the Galactic Republic, and Darth Malak's Sith Empire. Nord periodically taunted the Mandalorian Canderous Ordo, who was also an employee of Davik Kang. Darth Malak's [[Sith apprentice/Legends|Sith apprentice]], Darth Bandon, disliked Nord and viewed him as pathetic. Bandon expressed this opinion to Revan when the Sith confronted the Jedi. ==Equipment== Calo Nord used two [[Mandalorian heavy blaster]]s as his primary weapons, but he was capable of fighting with a [[Vibroblade/Legends|vibroblade]] when pressed. Nord also carried four [[Stun grenade/Legends|stun grenades]] and two thermal detonators in his arsenal. He possessed a pair of [[Goggles/Legends|goggles]] that granted him low-light vision when he wore them and used a prototype [[Verpine prototype shield|energy shield]] of [[Verpine/Legends|Verpine]] design for extra protection during battle. Nord carried 2,000 credits on his person and employed a [[Calo Nord's datapad|datapad]], on which he kept a record of his thoughts and trophy kills. At some point during his life, Nord ordered a [[Calo Nord's battle armor|custom suit]] of [[battle armor]] that was based on heavily modified [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] designs. After the suit was completed, Nord murdered the [[Unidentified armorer (Calo Nord)|armorer]] to ensure his suit would remain unique. ==Behind the scenes== ===Character development=== [[File:Calonordconcept.jpg|thumb|right|150px|Concept art of Nord]] Calo Nord was first created by the developers at [[BioWare]] as a boss-like villain for the [[2003]] [[Wikipedia:Xbox|Xbox]] and [[Wikipedia:Personal computer|PC]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]''. A miniature of Nord was released in the ''[[Bounty Hunters (Star Wars Miniatures)]]'', an expansion set of [[Wizards of the Coast]]'s ''[[Star Wars Miniatures]]'' [[Roleplaying game|role-playing game]]. The set was released on [[September 23]], [[2006]].{{SWMiniCite|set=Bounty Hunters|pack=Calo Nord}} Unlike the in-game outcome, Nord's entry in the ''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]'' states that he died during the Sith bombardment of Taris. His entry in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', however, states that Nord was killed near [[Lehon/Legends|Rakata Prime]] during the [[Battle of Rakata Prime|final battle]] of the Jedi Civil War.{{CSWECite|II|389|Nord, Calo}} A blog entry entitled ''[[So Uncivilized: Great Gunslingers in Star Wars]]'', the first part of which was published on [[September 19]], [[2013]], confirmed that Calo Nord met his fate on Tatooine. When [[John Jackson Miller]]—the writer of the [[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic (comic series)|''Knights of the Old Republic comic series'']]—was asked if there any other ''Knights of the Old Republic'' video game characters that he wanted to include in the series, Miller mentioned that he regretted not putting Nord in the comic.{{Faraway|url=blog/2010/02/14/kotor-countdown-7-just-asked-questions|text=KOTOR Countdown #7: Just-Asked Questions, Part Two}} In [[June]] [[2013]], Calo Nord's outfit was added for male and female players to purchase in the Cartel Market in the video game ''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'' as part of the Archon's Contraband pack.{{TORweb|url=info/news/blog/20130611|text=Archon's Contraband Pack}} ===Player options in ''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic''=== The planet on which Revan battles Nord is determined by the order the five Star Maps are obtained. Nord may appear either on Tatooine, the [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]], the [[Selkath/Legends|Selkath]] homeworld of [[Manaan/Legends|Manaan]], or the ancient Sith tomb-world of [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]]. On Tatooine, Nord is accompanied by Rodian and Aqualish henchmen. Nord confronts Revan with Wookiees loyal to [[Chief/Legends|Chieftain]] [[Chuundar]] on Kashyyyk. On Manaan, Nord is accompanied by two [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'leks]]. Should Revan encounter Nord in the [[Hrakert Station]] on Manaan, Nord explains that he used an [[Submarine/Legends|underwater vessel]] to reach the station. On Korriban, Nord is accompanied by two students from the [[Sith Academy (Korriban)|Sith Academy]]. The first time Nord is encountered in gameplay is in Javyar's Cantina in the Tarisian Lower City. If the player attempts to speak to Nord, the bounty hunter simply replies to go away. If the player opts to keep speaking to Nord, the bounty hunter counts to three. Should Nord reach the end of his three-count, the bounty hunter attacks, and the player is unable to defeat him and will be killed. However, it is possible to escape death by quickly leaving the conversation, which Nord calls a smart move before leaving the cantina. Should Canderous Ordo accompany the player when Nord confronts Revan for the last time, Ordo will make note of the fact that Nord brought backup and taunt the bounty hunter. ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Calo Nord}} *''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'' {{1st}} *[[Cartel Market|''Star Wars: The Old Republic'' Cartel Market]] {{Mo}} *''[[Star Wars: Galaxy of Heroes]]'' ==Sources== [[File:Calo_Nord_figure.jpg|thumb|right|''Star Wars Miniatures'' figure of Nord]] *[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic'': Prima's Official Strategy Guide]] *{{SWMiniCite|set=Bounty Hunters|pack=Calo Nord}} *{{InsiderCite|99|Tales of the Bounty Hunters (article)}} *''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]'' *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *{{DB|character|calonord|Nord, Calo}} *''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic Encyclopedia]]'' *{{SW|url=news/so-uncivilized-great-gunslingers-in-star-wars-part-1|series=So Uncivilized: Great Gunslingers in Star Wars|text=Part 1|nolive=1}} *''[[The Bounty Hunter Code: From the Files of Boba Fett]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |cs=Calo Nord |de=Calo Nord |el=Κάλο Νορντ |es=Calo Nord |fr=Calo Nord |it=Calo Nord |nl=Calo Nord |pl=Calo Nord |fi=Calo Nord }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nord, Calo}} [[Category:Bounty hunters]] [[Category:Darth Revan's Sith Empire individuals]] [[Category:Enslaved individuals]] [[Category:Exchange members]] [[Category:Galactic Republic military personnel]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Hunters]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Mercenaries]]
